programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: NY

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. programme.desc: A black man is murdered in a jail cell by a confirmed racist - a crime the prison service blames on overcrowding. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A photo featuring a missing 19-year-old music student turns up in a charity shop donation bag on the anniversary of her disappearance 17 years earlier, prompting Sandra to suspect the work of a serial killer. However, as the team gets to work identifying and tracking down the other people in the picture, increasing pressure to hand over the investigation forces Sandra to consider her future, and she turns to a familiar face to help her make her decision. Amanda Redman stars, with Denis Lawson, Dennis Waterman and Nicholas Lyndhurst.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Post-mortem results show they were strangled 15 years apart and as investigations proceed, suspicion falls on a friend of one victim's family. Drama, starring Amanda Burton and Anthony Head.(n)

Feature-length episode of the drama, starring Amanda Burton.(n)

programme.desc: Luke arrives back home in a wheelchair, but quickly becomes frustrated and takes his anger out on David. Charlie tries to avoid his daughter Charlotte when she returns from Italy and Nathan convinces Alice to keep their relationship secret - a move which drives her to accept a date with Joe.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: In a bid to raise some cash for Christmas, Tracey falls back on her typing skills, while Sharon reckons there is a gap in the market for her own unique brand of pet care.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Animal rights activists target the village, with Llewellyn falling victim to a paint bomb and the pub coming under threat, so Boycie organises patrol groups to protect Winderdown farm from attack. Meanwhile, Tyler wants to borrow money to buy equipment for his band, but Boycie refuses until his son completes a task to prove his business acumen.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Woollen Mills of Your Mind
programme.desc: Compo takes up marathon running, hoping that it will persuade Nora Batty to fall into his arms. Long-running rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Brian Wilde.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Who's Looking After the Cafe Then?

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective cancels a planned break in the company of his close friend Agatha Troy after being hauled into the assistant commissioner's office and ordered to investigate the death of a man found clad in monks' robes in a convent. The apparent murder coincides with the theft of a priceless chalice, testing the sleuth's powers of deduction to their full extent. Crime drama, guest starring David Haig, with Patrick Malahide.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The detectives are asked to carry out a preliminary investigation into a corrupt senior officer who may have suppressed evidence when a notorious crime boss was put away for murdering a prominent doctor. However, Sasha feels torn because she is almost certain that eight years earlier the convict in question also killed her CID partner. Meanwhile, Steve gets a shock when his estranged son turns up at work to see him. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Sam Ryan investigates the grisly discovery of a severed arm following a traffic collision, assisted by new morgue colleague Leo Dalton. As DCI Deacon arrives at the scene, Sam notices she is expressing an unusual interest in one of the bodies, leading her to believe her colleague could be withholding an important piece of evidence. Meanwhile, new junior pathologist Harry Cunningham faces a trying time when he finds out his first autopsy will be performed on a child.(n)

Her parents have spent the past two months living in hospital accommodation waiting for her to receive emergency surgery before they can take her home - but the procedure will only be possible when her weight reaches two kilograms. A couple enter the final preparations for their home delivery, and a mother who suffered severe post-natal depression after the birth of her first son gives birth to her third child.(n)

But on the day, Joe refuses to leave his room, the pressure of being the centre of attention proving too much for him. Rebecca comes to his aid and shows him that the walk is not a thing to be feared, and in turn gains the confidence to talk about her feelings concerning her pregnancy and the possibility that her child may also be autistic.(n)

programme.desc: Andy and Danielle have moved into a new home in Teignmouth, Devon, and are desperate for a garden that reminds them of their wonderful holidays in Morocco. programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: With the sun shining and the flowers blooming, summer is a season of splendid abundance, and the long hours of daylight make life burst out in a riot of activity. However, in certain regions there can be sweltering heat. As this documentary reveals, for a range of species, from sneaky ringtailed lemurs to battling ibex and penguins to colour-changing lizards, summer is a time when the living is not always easy. Narrated by Andrew Scott.(n)

programme.desc: Worthing couple Emma and Josh want to transform their overgrown plot into a family garden for their son Noah, who has Down's syndrome. Monty Don helps them learn about different sensory plants - only for rain to jeopardise the project. In Hitchin, Aletheia is determined to create a neighbourhood vegetable patch and a trip to Cranbrook Community Garden in London proves it is possible - but can she persuade the other locals to join in?(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the TV appearances made over the years by Hollywood star Robert Mitchum, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career.(n)

programme.title: The Magnificent Ambersons (1942)
programme.desc: A successful car manufacturer is reunited with his lost love, now a wealthy high society widow, and hopes to give their relationship a second chance. Her selfish son strives to keep them apart, as industrial progress leaves the whole family facing an uncertain future. Period drama, written, directed and narrated by Orson Welles, starring Joseph Cotten and Agnes Moorehead.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Bletchley Park in Buckinghamshire, where antique experts Charlie Ross and Mark Stacey find an incredible autograph book and a beautiful tea set.(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The keepers battle to save the life of a newborn reindeer calf that is too weak to suckle from its mother. A nest-cam is used to monitor a pair of rainbow lorikeet eggs.(n)

programme.title: Little Ships: The Miracle of Dunkirk
programme.desc: Dan Snow tells the story of the boats that played their part in lifting thousands of Allied troops off the beaches of Dunkirk in the week straddling late May and early June, 1940. Meeting some of the owners, he finds out what happened to the 700-strong flotilla of trawlers, pleasure craft and lifeboats and follows preparations to mark the 70th anniversary of the event as 50 of the surviving vessels return to France.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: A new format of the annual wildlife series in keeping with Covid-19 lockdown conditions, which sees the presenters broadcasting live from locations around the UK. Chris Packham is in the New Forest, Iolo William is in the heart of west Wales, and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver project near her home in Cornwall. In this first episode, they are joined by guest presenter Steve Backshall at his home on the banks of the Thames. Plus, Michaela Strachan calls live from South Africa to present some of her favourite moments from past series.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the history of an 18th-century sea captain's house near Bristol's docks with links to the slave trade. Bristol was a major port in the transportation of slaves from Africa's Guinea Coast to the Caribbean, and both the man who built the house and its first full-time resident were heavily involved in the trade. David uncovers stories of piracy, abandoned babies, a household slave who made a daring escape from the property, and a protest on the doorstep as the abolitionist movement gained momentum.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Dotty, Russell Kane, Richard Madeley, Steph McGovern and Steve Pemberton go for dinner together in this extended episode. They must place their bank card in the centre of the restaurant table. As the food is served and the drinks flow, they play a series of fun and revealing games, and if they win, they can retrieve their card. The person whose card remains at the end of the evening must pick up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk: The Soldiers' Story
programme.desc: Survivors of the British Expeditionary Force recall their reaction to the German army's blitzkrieg on May 10, 1940, which saw Holland and Belgium overrun. Few of these soldiers had experienced modern mechanised warfare or even been abroad before, so the environment of death, dive-bombing and fear came as a shattering blow, both physically and mentally.(n)

programme.title: 54 Hours: The Gladbeck Hostage Crisis
programme.desc: The situation escalates as the runaway robbers hijack a bus full of people in Gladbeck city centre. Conclusion of the fact-based crime drama about a hostage crisis that shocked Germany in the summer of 1988, starring Sascha Alexander Gersak and Alexander Scheer.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The ten remaining home sewers return to the nation's most famous sewing room for children's week, and are asked to make a classic smocked dress in the pattern challenge. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers let their imaginations run wild, turning sleeping bags into food shaped fancy dress. Finally, the contestants are asked to make a pair of dungarees for a little boy or girl.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison come up with virtual reality designs to transform a Halifax couple's 19th-century home. When owner Paula developed MS, it became vital to make the house more wheelchair accessible - but her partner Jeremy also sees an opportunity to give it a stylish new look. Laura's concept makes the most of the views of the garden and uses clever recycling tricks to stretch the budget, while Robert deploys off-the-shelf materials to making the place more accessible.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: How animals and plants deal with the changes in the environment that occur every autumn. As summer ends, animals have to prepare for the winter ahead. Chipmunks and beavers hurry to hoard their winter supplies before the weather grows colder, while musk oxen and beetles find they only have one more chance to mate before the year is out and young gannets face the first dangerous challenge of their lives. Narrated by Andrew Scott.(n)

programme.desc: Teddington woman Jennifer enlists her sisters' help to create an Italian-themed garden in memory of their parents. However, growing citrus trees, olive trees and vines in a leafy London suburb is easier said than done. In Billericay, Karensa dreams of turning a messy strip of back garden into an Alice in Wonderland playground for her daughter, complete with chessboard patio, heart-shaped lawn and teapot water feature. The only problem is, she has little idea how to go about it. Can Monty bring her closer to her fantasy?(n)

programme.desc: The prolific actor discusses some of the performances that have defined his career, including his roles in the Lord of the Rings trilogy and the Hammer studio's Dracula films.(n)

programme.desc: A man remarries seven years after his explorer wife's disappearance - but the last thing he expects is for her to suddenly reappear. It turns out she has been stranded on a desert island, and he is tempted to return to her - until he discovers she was not alone. Screwball comedy, starring Cary Grant and Irene Dunne.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies astrologer Russell Grant as he visits places from his childhood, including the church where he was a choirboy and the flat where he lived with his parents.(n)

programme.title: The Other Side of Dunkirk
programme.desc: The story behind the famed Second World War evacuation, as seen from the perspectives of other countries, shattering popular misconceptions about the operation and revealing a power struggle in the Nazi high command. Dunkirk has always been a source of bitterness in Anglo-French relations, with the British accusing their allies of cowardice and the French alleging that their soldiers were left to die. However, new evidence reveals both claims are far from the truth. Narrated by Tim Pigott-Smith.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams are live again close to home in the New Forest, West Wales and Cornwall, and cameras follow the fortunes of bird families in the Somerset levels and in the wild and rugged Brecon Beacons. With no boat traffic on the Thames, Steve Backshall witnesses beautiful aquatic insect hatches providing food for a host of birds such as swallows and red kites, while intrepid sound recordist Gary Moore records the incredible Urban Dawn Chorus of songbirds which have been such a feature of Spring 2020.(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: As Max tells his family that Clara has broken off their engagement, the Liebermanns are shocked by an act of violence committed by Leah's son Daniel. When bruises are found on the boy's body, Max persuades Oskar that the military academy his nephew attends should be investigated. The learn that one of the pupils died in an accident 10 days previously and when Max finds a coded note in Daniel's things he begins to suspect some sinister goings on at the academy. Crime drama, starring Matthew Beard.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe explores the concept of monogamy in this funny and lighthearted film, created using both live action and animation. Throughout the film, Sara discovers what makes people tick when it comes to matters of the heart, and why you should never underestimate the allure of a saxophone.(n)

programme.title: What's the Matter with Tony Slattery?
programme.desc: Tony Slattery was one of the most gifted TV comedians of the late 1980s and early 1990s, only to disappear from screens in 1996 amid rumours of a massive breakdown. As he approaches 60, he has been touring the country with a show that explores his past and his mental health. Although depression was diagnosed, he and his partner Mark Hutchinson are convinced he may have bipolar disorder, so Tony wants to have his mental health reassessed. But it will mean opening up about his addictions and a painful event in his childhood.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel

Enterpreneur Duncan Bannatyne, designer Zandra Rhodes, comedian Paul 'Chuckle' Elliott, cricket legend Henry Blofeld, former EastEnder John Altman, actresses Britt Ekland and Susie Blake and singer Barbara Dickson arrive at their new home by the sea in Puducherry, and while Britt struggles with the heat and Paul misses his missus, Susie, Barbara and Zandra bond over a bit of traditional Indian cooking.(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Mehreen Baig and a team of experts explore four spectacular coastlines linked by the Irish Sea, and meet the people who call them home. Each programme features people local to each region, as they fully immerse in the coastal way of life, from tackling unique jobs to experiencing magical close encounters with wildlife. The first focuses on the Merseyside coast, which stretches from Sefton Sands in the north to the Wirral in the south, via the port city of Liverpool.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how animals all over the world rise to the challenge of surviving the winter. While some creatures face an up-hill struggle to survive, a colder climate plays to the advantage of others, who quickly set about making use of inventive means to seize the huge opportunities the season brings. Caribou become 'ice road' travellers as the ground under-foot becomes slippery, and stoats build their own fur beds, snow monkeys set about finding warm baths, and even Emperor penguins - who are build to face extreme low temperatures - find their own way to endure the world's most savage winter conditions. Narrated by Andrew Scott.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Brighton, amateur horticulturists Helen and Dax have their hands full with twins, one of who has special needs. Their current garden is a building site, but armed with advice from presenter Monty Don they attack the challenge head on, and a trip to the Chelsea Flower Show also proves useful. Meanwhile in Buckinghamshire, Valerie's 'concrete driveway' of a garden has her stumped - she has no idea how to plant in or on it. A visit to an inspirational pottery puts her back on track, but with so many choices can she decide on the right ones?(n)

programme.title: Mr Blandings Builds His Dream House (1948)
programme.desc: A city couple set out to build a home in the wilds of rural Connecticut. They discover they have bitten off more than they can chew thanks to a host of legal problems and an apparently endless stream of cowboy workmen and high-priced materials. Comedy, starring Cary Grant, Myrna Loy, Melvyn Douglas and Reginald Denny.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: As a child, Alistair McGowan loved school lunches, especially the puddings. His mother's cooking was equally memorable and innovative, providing plenty of inspiration for the dishes that Brian Turner creates for Alistair. Together they revisit evocative places from Alistair's childhood, from Worcestershire's plum orchards to his old school, where sights and smells take him straight back to his youth. In his childhood home, we discover how Alistair realised his gift for impersonation as he switches effortlessly into some of the voices that helped him make his name.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At St Albans Cathedral and Abbey, Hertfordshire, Claire Rawle discovers an arts and crafts bowl that may have belonged to George Bernard Shaw's wife, and Jonathan Pratt finds a drawing by the artist Laura Knight. Presenter Paul Martin heads underground as he explores Royston Cave, which boasts medieval carvings.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble present as a breeding pair of rare Amur tigers arrive at the park, an oryx gives keepers the run around and the giraffes move into their summer pastures.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary and her guests examine the links between risk, culture and creativity. Director Lee Daniels explains why he thinks the current shutdown in Hollywood could be a radical creative opportunity for film-makers, and photographer Don McCullin questions the worth of a career spent risking his own life to alert the world to the suffering of warfare. Comedian and 'guilty feminist' Deborah Frances-White joins Mary and David Spiegelhalter, Professor of the Public Understanding of Risk, to disentangle the complexities of emerging into life after lockdown, and artists Gillian Wearing and Michael Landy sign off the show with their first collaboration in more than 20 years.(n)

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Historian Richard Holmes describes the events of May 1940, when dozens of small-scale actions combined to delay the Germans taking Dunkirk, and visits the beaches and breakwaters from which thousands of British troops narrowly escaped capture.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Live from their home bases in the New Forest, Cornwall, West Wales and the River Thames, Chris Packham, Gillian Burke, Iolo Williams and Steve Backshall share the latest stories from the wildlife cameras. Chris reveals how fish have more ways of making sound than any other vertebrate on the planet, Iolo discusses the intimate secrets of the bird families he has been following in his own back garden, and Michaela Strachan relives the dramatic story of a very skilful stoat in Minsmere in 2016. There is also the extraordinary spectacle of the firework sea anemone, and a rare reef made by beautiful peacock worms.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the return of the quiz with a difference, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers she finds most interesting. The show has now reached the letter R, and begins with a rather Rude episode. Joining Sandi are John Barrowman, Phill Jupitus, Aisling Bea and regular panellist Alan Davies.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Comedy written by Iain Morris and Damon Beesley - creators of The Inbetweeners - following the adventures of three young footballers, their Italian manager and eccentric chairman. New signing Mattie Sullivan arrives at the club, but a meeting with chairman Mark Crane leads him to wonder if he's been signed by mistake. As if that wasn't bad enough, club hard-man Petey Brooks takes a dislike to him and legendary Italian manager Cesare is nowhere to be found. Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Will Arnett, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Another leading performer on the stand-up circuit plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by fellow comedians ready to share their light-hearted views on modern life.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull continues their travels around Britain, this time seeking out the birthplaces of the inventions that fuelled the nation's industrial infrastructure. Their first stop is the Royal Institution in London, where they examine how the Davy lamp saved the lives of thousands of coal miners, then pay a visit to the preserved laboratory of Michael Faraday, another of their scientific heroes, whose research on electricity paved the way for its application as a ubiquitous energy supply.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an image of crashing waves in the moonlight, and demonstrates what can be achieved when painting on to a black canvas.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Sharon Eyal - A Basic Instinct
programme.desc: Following Sharon Eyal and Gai Behar create, rehearse and premiere a new dance, performed on the top of a multistorey car park in Peckham.(n)

programme.title: Chemistry - A Volatile History
programme.desc: Professor of Theoretical Physics Jim Al-Khalili charts the development of the Periodic Table and follows in the footsteps of the pioneers who discovered the elements that form the building blocks of the world.(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley examines how George II had to adapt to a growing middling rank in society - who were no longer content with being subjects. Affairs of the state began to be openly discussed in coffee houses, and the king and his ministers were mocked in satire - with George becoming an easy target due to his frequent absences. His wife Caroline went on to popularise a medical breakthrough against smallpox, but it was their son Frederick Prince of Wales who had the popularity the monarchy needed to survive in the modern era.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: A compendium of three short plays written and performed in isolation - Viral by James Graham, aboput three teenagers in pursuit of the next internet craze, Penny by Charlene James, starring Lennie James as a man living in a hotel wrestling with his own survival, and Going Forward by John Donnelly, in which a team leader takes drastic measures to ensure targets are met.(n)

programme.title: A Year in an English Garden: Flicker & Pulse
programme.desc: Brian McClave and Tom Wichelow's striking and poignant portrayal of time passing in a beautiful walled garden in the south-east of England. Using real-time and time-lapse footage, the film explores the relationship between the seasons and the plants and people who work within the walls of the garden. Locked into the clock of the solar system, the garden performs its annual display, guided by those passionately engaged with its soil.(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook examines sci-fi's obsession with alien invasion, from all-out assault to sinister hidden threats. He explores how it has reflected real-life anxieties - whether they be the challenge to Victorian imperial power in HG Wells's The War of the Worlds, the Cold War paranoia in Invasion of the Body Snatchers or more recent concerns about racism and immigration in 2009 movie District 9. He also celebrates one of the most famous alien invaders of all time - the Dalek. Featuring contributions by former Doctor Who showrunner Steven Moffat and former star David Tennant, actor Richard Dreyfuss (Close Encounters of the Third Kind), Independence Day director Roland Emmerich and special effects supervisors Phil Tippett (Star Wars, Jurassic Park, RoboCop) and Douglas Trumbull (2001: A Space Odyssey, Close Encounters, Blade Runner).(n)

programme.title: The Art of Gothic: Britain's Midnight Hour
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon continues his examination of England's Gothic Revival, focusing on the influence of the Industrial Revolution on Gothic art and literature, which became both backward and forward looking. While Mary Shelley warned of the dangers of how science could get out of control in her novel Frankenstein, Sir Giles Gilbert Scott used Gothic architecture to memorialise Prince Albert as a medieval hero. Meanwhile, poets indulged in hallucinatory drugs to reach new Gothic heights.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an image of crashing waves in the moonlight, and demonstrates what can be achieved when painting on to a black canvas.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Sharon Eyal - A Basic Instinct
programme.desc: Following Sharon Eyal and Gai Behar create, rehearse and premiere a new dance, performed on the top of a multistorey car park in Peckham.(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley examines how George II had to adapt to a growing middling rank in society - who were no longer content with being subjects. Affairs of the state began to be openly discussed in coffee houses, and the king and his ministers were mocked in satire - with George becoming an easy target due to his frequent absences. His wife Caroline went on to popularise a medical breakthrough against smallpox, but it was their son Frederick Prince of Wales who had the popularity the monarchy needed to survive in the modern era.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates how to paint a statuesque ridge of peaks overlooking a little country home by the cove.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Ballet Black - The Waiting Game
programme.desc: Documentary that explores the work of Artistic Director Cassa Pancho as she nurtures aspiring young dancers and gains recognition for her company's collaborations with world-class choreographers. It also follows the creative process of South African dancer and choreographer Mthuthuzeli November, as he creates a dynamic new ballet inspired in equal measure by Samuel Beckett's seminal Waiting for Godot and his own African rhythms and language.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the history of the village of Kibworth, Leicestershire, from the 19th century to the present day, including the Penny Concerts of the 1880s. He accompanies local children on a school trip to France, where the pupils commemorate relatives who died in the First World War. Michael Wood also reveals the story of the Home Guard and the bombing of the village in 1940, and how modern times arrived in the village.(n)

programme.title: Men at the Barre - Inside the Royal Ballet
programme.desc: Award-winning filmmaker Richard Macer is invited to film with a golden generation of talented young male dancers at the Royal Ballet to find out what it takes to be an elite dancer. Throughout the film, Richard learns that in the past, the man's role was just to lift the ballerina into the air, but now, the top male dancers have fanbases that even rival the best ballerina's. He also explores changes in choreography, which is starting to reflect masculinity in different ways, mirroring the changing perception of what it is to be man outside in the wider world.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Stories of love, triumph and uncertainty during the time of coronavirus, focusing on the lives of couples, NHS workers, families and teenagers whose lives have changed dramatically. This episode features Romantic Distancing, written by Tim Price and starring Ines De Clercq and Arthur Darvill, Safer at Home, written by Anna Maloney and starring Gemma Arterton, Geraldine James and Rory Keenan, and House Party, written by April De Angelis and starring Patricia Allison, Risteard Cooper, Gabriel Harland, Cecilia Noble, James Norton, Rhashan Stone, Meera Syal, Olivia Williams and Fenella Woolgar.(n)

programme.title: BalletBoyz Deluxe
programme.desc: Acclaimed BalletBoyz celebrate their 20th Anniversary with their new show, Deluxe. The first act, Bradley 4:18, choreographed by Punchdrunk's Maxine Doyle, is inspired by the lyrics and story of spoken word artist Kate Tempest's Pictures on a Screen, about Bradley, a seemingly successful young man struggling to connect with the world around him. It is set to a score by Mercury Award nominated and Ivors Academy Award winning London-based composer, arranger and alto-saxophonist Cassie Kinoshi.(n)

programme.title: BalletBoyz Deluxe
programme.desc: The second act of BalletBoyz new show, Deluxe, featuring choreography by Xie Xin and music by Jiang Shao-feng, which sees the company express new styles in contrast to their familiar athletic and strength-oriented work.(n)

programme.title: Dance on Film: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Acclaimed dancer Carlos Acosta introduces a new generation of film makers who use b-boying, ballet and contemporary dance to tell their stories. Subjects range from dancing in a bingo hall, acid attacks, body image and wellbeing and the mystical world of baby eels.(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores how the public's view of Roman Britain has changed over the decades, examining events from the initial invasion, through to the construction of Hadrian's Wall and the eventual collapse of the empire, drawing on the work of archaeologists and historians and using 50 years of BBC archive footage. With contributions from Charlotte Higgins, Adrian Goldsworthy, Neil Faulkner and Jo Quinn.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates how to paint a statuesque ridge of peaks overlooking a little country home by the cove.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Ballet Black - The Waiting Game
programme.desc: Documentary that explores the work of Artistic Director Cassa Pancho as she nurtures aspiring young dancers and gains recognition for her company's collaborations with world-class choreographers. It also follows the creative process of South African dancer and choreographer Mthuthuzeli November, as he creates a dynamic new ballet inspired in equal measure by Samuel Beckett's seminal Waiting for Godot and his own African rhythms and language.(n)

programme.title: Men at the Barre - Inside the Royal Ballet
programme.desc: Award-winning filmmaker Richard Macer is invited to film with a golden generation of talented young male dancers at the Royal Ballet to find out what it takes to be an elite dancer. Throughout the film, Richard learns that in the past, the man's role was just to lift the ballerina into the air, but now, the top male dancers have fanbases that even rival the best ballerina's. programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a tropical seascape - an exotic ocean masterpiece complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze.(n)

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.sub-title: Danceworks: Maria Pagés - An Ode to Flamenco
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of flamenco star María Pagés, as she prepares a haunting new choreography for a performance in her birthplace of Seville.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the third episode, cameras unveil stories behind some of the planet's most intriguing colours, including the lights of the aurora, which tower in vertical streaks hundreds of kilometres high. Elsewhere, cameras also capture vast phytoplankton blooms in the ocean, and millions of rapeseed flowers in bloom in China's Yunnan province.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Two short plays written and performed in isolation: Grounded, by Duncan Macmillan, tells the story of a woman who is worried about her elderly parents' cavalier attitude to the virus. However, when she is overcome by the stress of her work, it seems that she is the one who needs looking after. Katherine Parkinson, Alison Steadman, Michael Elwyn and Arlo Dollimore star. Fear Fatigue, by Prasanna Puwanarajah, is based on conversations with NHS workers in March 2020, and documents the feelings and fears of frontline staff in the days and weeks before the coronavirus lockdown. Starring Paterson Joseph, Rory Kinnear, Esh Alladi, Natalie Dew, Anna Madeley and Jodie McNee.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Three new lockdown dramas. Kat and Zaccy, written by Deboran Bruce, is about a son ignoring his mother's emotional pleas to come home from university during lockdown. Monica Dolan and Alex Lawther star. The Unexpected Expert, by Matilda Ibini, tells the story of a woman who worries that the crisis will have a huge impact on her social care package. Saida Ahmed, Lucianne McEvoy and Golda Rosheuvel star. The Night After, by Josh Azouz, stars Kathryn Hunter and Marcello Magni as a couple in their sixties who try to make contact with the outside world.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.desc: Part one of two. As rumours circulated that the Soviets were preparing to send an unmanned spacecraft to the moon, Nasa quickly developed the Gemini programme, sending astronauts into Earth orbit to practice critical manoeuvres for the eventual trip to the Moon.(n)

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian discovers French plans to invade Britain by balloon, the subterranean fortress built for troops in the 19th century when invasion paranoia was at fever pitch, and examines the rich legacy of the Huguenots.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Carl's accordion routines are hindering George's puppet show rehearsals. George must decide whether to keep Carl and risk the show, or replace him and risk his friendship.(n)

programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog.(n)

programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, presented by Alex Jones and Angellica Bell.(n)

programme.title: The Great Indoors
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman returns to celebrate the people going above and beyond to keep everyone safe, fed and well while they stay inside during these difficult times.(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Clap for Our Carers
programme.desc: Live coverage as people around the UK applaud health service workers battling the coronavirus pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon tries to distract herself but gets more than she bargained for when she drops off some leftover baby items at Karen's and finds herself watching baby Kayden. Phil puts pressure on Ben to not mess up the job, unaware his son has done his own secret deal, while Jean dresses up in the hope of impressing Phil and persuading him to let Stacey come home. Keegan's dreams come crashing down when he admits to Iqra that he did not sort out insurance on the vandalised food truck.(n)

programme.title: Silicon Glen: From Ships to Microchips
programme.desc: The story of IBM's facility in Spango Valley, Greenock, which provided the region with thousands of jobs at the cutting edge of modern technology from the post-war period to its closure in 2018, as well as drawing investments to Scotland from all over the world. The programme features the stories of former employees, and discovers the role the workforce played in the moon landing and the making of classic film 2001: A Space Odyssey.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Alison organises a sponsored walk to raise money for Joe's class. Volunteering brings the family back together, and also provides Alison with a chance to get to know Heather better. But on the day, Joe refuses to leave his room, the pressure of being the centre of attention proving too much for him. Rebecca comes to his aid and shows him that the walk is not a thing to be feared, and in turn gains the confidence to talk about her feelings concerning her pregnancy and the possibility that her child may also be autistic.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: Baby Elsie has been born eight weeks premature, weighs just 860 grams and was born with a hole in her heart. Her parents have spent the past two months living in hospital accommodation waiting for her to receive emergency surgery before they can take her home - but the procedure will only be possible when her weight reaches two kilograms. A couple enter the final preparations for their home delivery, and a mother who suffered severe post-natal depression after the birth of her first son gives birth to her third child.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the seven remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for reduce, reuse and recycle week. Joe Lycett kicks the group off with a pattern for a man's bomber jacket, which must be pieced together from four second-hand women's garments. In the transformation challenge, the sewers are tasked with changing laundry bags into a stylish and wearable garment, before finally being tasked to use old knitwear to make a made to measure jumper dress.(n)

programme.desc: The Abbey Grove School election gets under way, and Alfie makes it his mission to prove Miss Gulliver wrong after she implies that none of his class members is suitable. programme.title: Richard and Jaco Take On the World
programme.desc: A personal exploration of autism with actor Richard Mylan and his autistic son Jaco. The pair journey from Wales to America and back again, exploring what it means to be autistic and finding out how places from a local barbershop to a national stadium can become more inclusive for autistic people. They visit Sesame Place theme park in Pennsylvania, where Jaco has a chance to meet Julia, Sesame Street's first character with autism.(n)

programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists.(n)

programme.title: Bad Education
programme.desc: The Abbey Grove students prepare for their biggest football match of the school year, and Fraser decides Alfie should become coach of the team when the PE teacher disappears. This poses Alfie a number of problems, including the fact that the opponents are coached by his old games teacher and arch-enemy Mr Humpage. Guest starring Rupert Vansittart and Chris Kamara.(n)

programme.title: Bad Education
programme.desc: The Abbey Grove School election gets under way, and Alfie makes it his mission to prove Miss Gulliver wrong after she implies that none of his class members is suitable. Meanwhile, Fraser is suspended following an unfortunate incident involving a provocative internet viral. Starring Jack Whitehall, Sarah Solemani and Mathew Horne.(n)

programme.title: Richard and Jaco Take On the World
programme.desc: A personal exploration of autism with actor Richard Mylan and his autistic son Jaco. The pair journey from Wales to America and back again, exploring what it means to be autistic and finding out how places from a local barbershop to a national stadium can become more inclusive for autistic people. They visit Sesame Place theme park in Pennsylvania, where Jaco has a chance to meet Julia, Sesame Street's first character with autism.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.desc: A Letchworth couple receive a surprise £4,500 garden makeover as a thank you from their son - a challenge that Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers find difficult to resist.(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Clap for Our Carers
programme.desc: Live coverage as people around the UK applaud health service workers battling the coronavirus pandemic.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.desc: When Penfold scores a date with superstar Scarlett Johamster, Danger Mouse and the Professor provide conflicting advice.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Science is brought to life with fun experiments that can be tried at home, starting with how to fake a gory wound. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.sub-title: Dotaidh an Leumadair Ballach (Dotty the Spotted Dolphin)
programme.desc: Dotty the spotted dolphin reveals facts about her species, including how they live in groups called pods, how they work together to hunt for fish and the threats they face from great white sharks and fishing nets.(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Documentary following Donald Macsween - aka Sweeny - as he tries to become a full-time crofter. In the first programme, he prepares to go into egg production with 300 chickens. However, as the day of the chickens' arrival looms with a trip to the mainland to collect them, the hen house is far from finished.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about uses of water, including ice baths, cliff jumping and divination.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: It's games week in Uist and the bakers are busy preparing rolls that are much needed for the hundreds who will be heading to the machair both in the North and the South.(n)

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie Macleod travels between Glasgow and Barra tackling a number of interesting DiY projects which showcase his many talents. In this programme, the expert is surprised to find himself at his sister Margaret's house where a pile of wood is waiting to be transformed into garden decking. A couple of days later Donnie's finished, but what will Margaret make of it?(n)

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Ross Coupar and Tom Oakes perform one of their sets rooted in Scottish and Irish traditions.(n)

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: The Scottish-based outfit Dallahan.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis presents a new music series for our times, offering musicians an opportunity to perform directly from their homes to viewing fans.(n)

programme.title: Ameireaga air Film (America in Colour)
programme.desc: Advances in technology in the first half of the 20th century, following three of the most influential figures - Henry Ford, Harvey Firestone and Thomas Edison.(n)

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Benedict Morris, Pablo Lafuente and Ryan Murphy perform at Na Trads in 2019.(n)

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Uisdean and Roddy head out of the kitchen, one to the country and one to the sea, returning with venison sausages for a casserole and a wealth of seafood for a platter.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson steps into Chris Tarrant's shoes to host the first edition of the revival of the quiz. Contestants sit in the famous chair to answer a maximum of 15 questions, hoping to win £1million and join original millionaires Judith Keppel, David Edwards, Robert Brydges, Pat Gibson and Ingram Wilcox. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Cocksure 17-year-old Bethany is barely out of the test centre before getting into a game of chicken. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David is chased by an angry mob and cornered in an alleyway, while the doctor warns Leanne that it is too soon to be sure, but Oliver may have suffered brain damage. Tommy Orpington assures James he did the right thing coming out, and Chesney urges Gemma to share her vlog online.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: Manchester-born brothers Ryan, Adam and Scott Thomas embark on an epic journey to trace their family roots in India, joined by their dad Dougie, whose father was born in Mumbai. However, the trio have never visited the country they descended from. In the fifth episode, Adam attempts to master an ancient sport when he and Scott get a taste of Indian parklife. Elsewhere, Ryan and Dougie make a shock discovery about Dougie's grandmother, before the group join in with the festivities for the country's biggest party for Diwali.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul helps to feed a pair of week-old kittens whose mother has abandoned them. However, as the kittens grow older, the team discover that one of them has a neurological problem affecting his movement. Later, Paul meets a group of dogs from a puppy farm who have had very little human interaction in the past, and need help learning how to socialise.(n)

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Mercer and his associates are tried for their roles in the killing of 11-year-old Rhys Jones, but the verdict fails to bring closure for the boy's parents Melanie and Steve as the fractures in their relationship come to a head. Meanwhile an unexpected setback leaves Det Supt Dave Kelly with a tough decision to make. The concluding part of the fact-based drama centred on the devastating impact of a child's murder amid a wave of gang violence in Liverpool, starring Stephen Graham, Sinead Keenan and Brían F O'Byrne.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection of mishaps sent in by viewers, featuring accidents filmed using the latest technology only - no camcorder clips or pixelated phone videos in sight. Tonight, a man is accidentally tasered, a couple set their kitchen on fire, and a toddler in an electric car runs amok in a shopping centre.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Scammers: Hunting the Lockdown Criminals - Tonight
programme.desc: There has been a spike in internet fraud - from fake goods and services to con artists impersonating the authorities through emails, texts, calls and even appearing at people's front doors. Paul Connolly investigates the unscrupulous crooks using the lockdown to prey on others.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty presents as eight celebrity families take part in the knockout tournament, hoping to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The first episode is a battle of the soaps, as Coronation Street's Lucy Fallon (Bethany Platt) and her family take on former EastEnders star Shaun Williamson (Barry Evans) and his clan. Two new rounds have been added to this celebrity spin on the format - including an Observation round featuring cartoon images of Anne that put their memories to the test.(n)

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.sub-title: The Good, The Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Martin learns about animal exploitation - the good, the bad and the ugly. The actor visits Sri Lanka, where fishing for manta rays is legal, but a turtle sanctuary offers hope. In Thailand, he learns more about silk production - as well as a more animal-friendly alternative - and on the Australian Friday Island, he is shown the delicate art of growing pearls. Finally, in the Abrolhos islands, Martin learns about the most valuable catch in Australia's waters.(n)

programme.title: Police Tapes
programme.sub-title: The Murder of Rhys Jones: Police Tapes
programme.desc: When 11-year-old Rhys Jones was shot dead on his way home from weekly football practice in 2007, his family was left shattered and police were left with a case that seemed impenetrable. But when certain names were mentioned again and again, the police planted secret bugs in the homes of these gang members. Here, Susanna Reid gains unprecedented access to the subsequent recordings, where those responsible admit their part and the lengths they would go to cover up the crime. She also talks to Rhys's parents, Mel and Steve, about the impact of this appalling crime.(n)

programme.desc: Kim Cattrall is the first guest as the chat show returns, with the actress revealing why she turned down Sex and the City 3 and talking about her relationship with the other cast members following rumoured feuds. programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, which sees five bankers competing to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of popular culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: ITV Evening News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection of mishaps sent in by viewers, featuring accidents filmed using the latest technology only - no camcorder clips or pixelated phone videos in sight. Tonight, a man is accidentally tasered, a couple set their kitchen on fire, and a toddler in an electric car runs amok in a shopping centre.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Scammers: Hunting the Lockdown Criminals - Tonight
programme.desc: There has been a spike in internet fraud - from fake goods and services to con artists impersonating the authorities through emails, texts, calls and even appearing at people's front doors. Paul Connolly investigates the unscrupulous crooks using the lockdown to prey on others.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty presents as eight celebrity families take part in the knockout tournament, hoping to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The first episode is a battle of the soaps, as Coronation Street's Lucy Fallon (Bethany Platt) and her family take on former EastEnders star Shaun Williamson (Barry Evans) and his clan. Two new rounds have been added to this celebrity spin on the format - including an Observation round featuring cartoon images of Anne that put their memories to the test.(n)

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.sub-title: The Good, The Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Martin learns about animal exploitation - the good, the bad and the ugly. The actor visits Sri Lanka, where fishing for manta rays is legal, but a turtle sanctuary offers hope. In Thailand, he learns more about silk production - as well as a more animal-friendly alternative - and on the Australian Friday Island, he is shown the delicate art of growing pearls. Finally, in the Abrolhos islands, Martin learns about the most valuable catch in Australia's waters.(n)

programme.title: Police Tapes
programme.sub-title: The Murder of Rhys Jones: Police Tapes
programme.desc: When 11-year-old Rhys Jones was shot dead on his way home from weekly football practice in 2007, his family was left shattered and police were left with a case that seemed impenetrable. But when certain names were mentioned again and again, the police planted secret bugs in the homes of these gang members. Here, Susanna Reid gains unprecedented access to the subsequent recordings, where those responsible admit their part and the lengths they would go to cover up the crime. She also talks to Rhys's parents, Mel and Steve, about the impact of this appalling crime.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: ITV News London

programme.desc: Kim Cattrall is the first guest as the chat show returns, with the actress revealing why she turned down Sex and the City 3 and talking about her relationship with the other cast members following rumoured feuds. She also opens up to Piers about her three marriages and discusses why she was forced to pull out of a West End play.(n)

Mike faces a stressful time as he struggles to cope with his wife's departure, and Tricia offers him a shoulder to cry on, but is beset by problems of her own after prescribing birth control to a 16-year-old. Meanwhile, Sergeant Craddock finds his efforts to prepare for an important visit hampered by a series of attacks on the station, and Vernon makes a startling discovery in his bathtub. programme.desc: When Mildred finds out that George only has a small endowment, she decides to do something about it. However, George is none too happy when he realises that he is worth more dead than alive. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George trades his car in for a battered caravan, and Mildred's sister and brother-in-law arrive, leading to problems in the bedroom. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy, Yootha Joyce, Norman Eshley and Sheila Fearn.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star.(n)

programme.desc: Part two of two. Carl Lomax's case comes to court, and a local reporter soon rakes up the dirt over Tricia's controversial decision to supply his girlfriend with birth-control drugs. programme.desc: Cora makes a discovery that throws the inheritance issue into chaos, and Mary is prompted to keep Matthew waiting for an answer to his proposal. Robert arranges for Mrs Patmore to see an eye specialist, and O'Brien digs up more compromising information about Bates, but the Earl's response comes as a surprise. Thomas leaves the Abbey and Mary exacts vengeance on Edith for her treachery, while the household is rocked by a shocking announcement. Drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo).(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.desc: A faulty TV aerial prompts George to borrow his neighbour's ladder - but relations become strained when the henpecked husband falls off the roof and starts pointing the finger of blame. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox.(n)

Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed.(n)

It seems there is an even darker side to the roguish wheeler-dealer than they suspected.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield residents are shocked when an MI5 agent turns up in the village showing more than just a passing interest in the activities of Vernon Scripps and his mysterious associate, a man who disembarked from a Russian trawler docked in Whitby harbour. programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It is November 1916, and the family and servants are getting the house ready for a concert to raise funds to treat more casualties from the front. Bates returns from London with a proposal for Anna, while Isobel informs Robert and Cora that Matthew is engaged and wants to bring his fiancee to Downton. O'Brien plays a trick on new maid Ethel after taking an instant dislike to her. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The death of Oxford don RG Cole leads to the shady underworld of unlicensed boxing and the involvement of academic Richard Helm. Investigations reveal the dead man's student Jack Roth was involved in a complex love triangle, and when the young man's rival is found dead in an apparent suicide, Lewis suspects foul play and discovers an old connection to Helm - along with a link to his former mentor Morse. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.sub-title: I Won't Dance, Don't Ask Me
programme.desc: Chrissy needs a partner for the annual staff dance, so Robin takes lessons from Mrs Roper. Flat-sharing comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home.
