programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Josh throws himself into his new job as a photojournalist with the Boston Citizen, but spends too much time socialising with lonely boss Mike Shelby and neglects Sabrina. Guest starring George Wendt.(n)

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina takes her friends on a spooky murder-mystery excursion aboard an old-fashioned train, little suspecting she's accidentally transported them into the Other Realm - a region hazardous to mortals.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Russell is forced to move into Timmy's apartment after his mother cuts him off, but begins to make friends with his new neighbours. Audrey offers to flirt with Jeff's boss to increase his chances of earning a bonus, and Adam tries to get Jen to dress more glamorously in bed.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Jeff asks his colleagues to sponsor Audrey in a 10k charity run, but struggles to raise funds having previously refused their own appeals for donations. programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Roxie and Sabrina land their own show on the campus station, but have trouble thinking of ways to fill the airwaves for two whole hours. Melissa Joan Hart stars.(n)

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Salem creates double trouble by accidentally cloning himself, and the god Mercury helps Sabrina's boyfriend Harvey overcome a skating injury - at a price.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox helps Ryder get creative on a job application. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel and Joe meet with their minister to discuss their relationship, and try to prove that everything between them is perfect. programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben and Riley try to make the difficult transition from friends to being a couple, which leads to an embarrassing moment for Riley. Elsewhere, Tucker helps Danny to get over his failed relationship. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley begins to wonder whether Ben owes it to himself to give family life with Angela and baby Emma a chance, while Bonnie tries to keep her age a secret when she dates a much younger man. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau, Chelsea Kane and Derek Theler.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj is upset after Lucy runs away in the middle of their date, leaving the gang to cheer him up. However, when Howard bumps into her, she admits she is incredibly shy. Meanwhile, Sheldon asks Penny to help with a new episode of Fun with Flags, and because he is impressed by her acting ability, the friends go to see her in a performance of A Streetcar Named Desire.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While the girls go to Disneyland and get made up as princesses, the guys have to promote science as a career option for women at Howard's old middle school. Meanwhile, Raj and Lucy have their first date in a library, where instead of speaking to each other they send text messages.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam joins a mail-order music club, aiming to assemble an enviable cassette tape collection. He takes advantage of the 12 tapes for a penny promotion by opening multiple accounts with the club using fake names to get as much music as possible, but has no intention of continuing the monthly contracts. However, when Beverly discovers the scam she blames Erica. Barry asks Murray for help when he realises Lainey's dad Bill doesn't like him.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Inspired to become a magician, Adam attempts to use his new magic act to attract girls at school, but is thwarted when Beverly insists on taking to the stage as his assistant. Murray is alarmed when Barry refuses to study for a college entrance exam with his peers.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: For his first task back in the field, Jeffords prepares to join Peralta and Boyle on a mission to infiltrate a gym where steroids are being sold illegally, but Peralta begins to have second thoughts about whether the sergeant is ready. Meanwhile, Santiago and Diaz try to determine who has burgled Gina's flat.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: While investigating a string of hotel robberies together, Peralta is upset to discover Santiago is considering leaving the precinct to work with his rival, the Vulture, in the special crimes unit. Holt and Jeffords try to think up some strategies to make the team more efficient. Featuring a guest appearance by Adam Sandler.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny offer Sheldon's old room to Raj after he is forced to move out of his apartment to save money, leading to unexpected conflict among the gang and some unwanted observations from Leonard's mother. Christine Baranski guests.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Penny and Raj settle into their new living arrangement, but Penny and Raj get along so well together that Leonard starts to feel left out. Sheldon expresses an interest in Amy's work and they begin to collaborate on a scientific project.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe wants to start a family and invites Nate and Gillian round, hoping their baby will be enough to convince Mel she is ready to handle the responsibility. Meanwhile, Lennox plays matchmaker for Zander and Ryder reveals plans to move to Louisiana.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: The family gathers around the laptop to video chat with Ryder in New Orleans on his 18th birthday, but the computer crashes before they get the chance to talk. Joe insists that he can fix it, but Mel is convinced they need to employ an expert to repair it. Dani asks Lennox for advice on matters of the heart but doesn't follow her words of wisdom. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie decides to set Ben up with her friend Bailey, and all seems to be going well until Ben realises his new girlfriend bears similarities to his mother. Meanwhile, Riley and Tucker take a car that belongs to Danny's father out for a spin. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben throws Emma a first birthday party at the ice-cream parlour where he and Riley worked as teenagers. However, he runs into an ex-girlfriend with a major secret. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Captain Holt is taken by surprise when his long-time nemesis, Deputy Chief Madeline Wuntch (guest star Kyra Sedgwick), arrives to carry out an inspection of his precinct. Peralta and Jeffords test the boundaries between 'work friendship' and 'real friendship' while they investigate a stabbing at a trendy milk bar. Boyle searches for a guest to take to his ex-wife's engagement party - will Diaz come to the rescue?(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad has time to kill when the arrival of the Serbian president's motorcade to Brooklyn is delayed, so Peralta launches the seventh annual Jimmy Jab Games, an eccentric series of races and contests held in the precinct building. Meanwhile, Jeffords and Holt must set up a new drug task force approved by Holt's nemesis, Madeline Wuntch.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: After getting telephone answering machines, Murray and Pop-Pop wage a message war on one another, so Erica, Barry and Pops try to reunite them at a hockey game.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tries to be a horror writer, but his efforts get him grounded on Halloween when Beverly discovers that she is the basis for the monster in his story. Erica forces Barry to attend a costume dance with her.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is devastated when his mother befriends Penny. Howard and Sheldon bond when they drive into the desert to shoot off a model rocket. Christine Baranski guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Raj turn to Bernadette for advice about their jealousy when Amy and Howard decide to collaborate on a new project. Penny and Leonard learn some new ways to manage Sheldon's irksome behaviour from an unlikely source.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks Favourites
programme.desc: Looking back at another classic episode of the soap. Brendan is a changed man as he openly shows affection towards Ste in Dublin, but there is darkness on the horizon.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dave Benson Phillips' food gets a grilling from Anthea Turner, Marcel Somerville, Amelia Dimoldenberg and AJ Odudu, so he tries to save his evening with a spot of clowning around.(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Rival Prodigy and Sir Isaac Neutron
programme.desc: Meemaw intervenes when Sheldon gets jealous of Dr Sturgis bonding with Paige, a rival 10-year-old genius. In the meantime, Mary invites Paige's family over for a play date.(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Crisis of Faith and Octopus Aliens
programme.desc: Following a tragic accident in the local community, Mary's faith is shaken. Seeing his mother doubt everything she believes, Sheldon becomes an unlikely ally. Comedy, starring Iain Armitage and Zoe Perry.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad members partner up for their next Halloween Heist - unaware they have to be handcuffed to each other. But that is just the start of their problems.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: Summer takes up knitting, and when her scarf gets caught in the garage door, it triggers a series of events leading up to the total destruction of the multiverse.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty

programme.title: Harley Quinn

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: When Morty complains that Rick's adventures are too frightening and traumatic, Rick allows him to lead their next expedition. Rick also provides the family with an inventive solution when they come to him with their trivial problems.(n)

programme.title: Robot Chicken
programme.desc: The Robot Chicken writers go back to work on a new season, but have to take Fury Road to get to the office. Beavis has moved on from his life with Butt-head, and the Robot Chicken Nerd is the latest guest in Westworld. Weird Al Yankovic guest stars. Animated comedy series, created by Seth Green and Matthew Senreich.(n)

programme.title: Robot Chicken
programme.desc: The Robot Chicken crew wonders whether Doc McStuffins can handle things in the emergency room. Investors are less than thrilled with Westworld's first generation of hosts, and the Scooby gang members tell each other how they really feel - with lethal results. Linda Cardellini, Sarah Michelle Gellar, Matthew Lillard and Freddie Prinze Jr guest star.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp shows viewers some quirky ways to dress a dining table, and reveals how to make a paper napkin rose and a cupcake bouquet. The presenter also demonstrates a recipe for chocolate bowls.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: A Derbyshire couple go searching the French region of Aude for a home where they can start married life. But while Faye has her heart set on a character property, Philip likes modern clean lines. With £150,000, can Sara Damergi find something they agree on?(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to the Cotswolds to see who can create the most profit on two old lamps, and also take a spin in a Devonshire fire engine.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien creates table lamps from old theatre lights and Henry Cole finds a treat for restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul take on a massive gardening project, expanding a lake and creating an al fresco dining area. Mariam and Johnno launch their chateau as an events venue.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Cambridgeshire to meet self-builders Sarah and Rob, who are aiming to construct a family home in the village where they first met as children.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the B&B competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park B&B in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive B&B Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final B&B visit. The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespie's farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the week's winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dance hall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.sub-title: London's Lost Route to the Sea
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go in search of the lost route between London and the sea, which has not been fully open for more than 200 years and requires four different boats to traverse now. They leave the Thames at Teddington and travel through the Surrey and Sussex countryside before arriving at Guildford, and a canal-side theatre where they have both regularly performed. The final leg of the trip brings them to the River Arun before reaching the English Channel.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Mariam and Johnno restore their chateau's original bread oven for a sourdough weekend. Angel goes bargain hunting to find a unique radiator cover for the chateau's boot room, while Emma and Paul complete the final room in the west wing and theme it after the renowned artist Alphonse Mucha.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets artists Indi and Rebecca, who are planning to construct a modern, larch-clad home on the Isle of Skye, with a second hand-crafted building alongside to use as their studio, all on a tight budget of £150,000. They have been saving for years, but making it happen will mean taking on the savage weather, not to mention the sceptical locals - and as if that weren't enough, most of the work is being done by one man, local builder Donald.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Mohammed, 64, who has been hit by a pizza delivery motorbike. Consultant Will is concerned that he may have a pelvic fracture, which could seriously affect his mobility and, if it hits a major artery, can be life threatening. There's also a profile of John, a 90-year-old who comes to St George's in south west London with his wife Iris and daughter Joan after his GP picked up on his abnormally low heart rate.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Coventry are called to a pregnant woman with severe stomach pains, where a potentially life-threatening ectopic pregnancy is suspected.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams compete against Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson. David Baddiel joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates another look back at TV shows from the 1970s, focusing on the comedies, documentaries and public information films that made people so anxious 40-plus years ago. Some of the famous shows covered include Man Alive and Brass Tacks, as well as The Goodies taking on the police and June Whitfield burning her bra. The show features first-hand accounts from the people who were making telly at the time, including Barry Cryer, Janet Street-Porter and Bill Oddie.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Mohammed, 64, who has been hit by a pizza delivery motorbike. Consultant Will is concerned that he may have a pelvic fracture, which could seriously affect his mobility and, if it hits a major artery, can be life threatening. There's also a profile of John, a 90-year-old who comes to St George's in south west London with his wife Iris and daughter Joan after his GP picked up on his abnormally low heart rate.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy to find out how supermarkets can sell pesto at an affordable price when the traditional ingredients are expensive, while Jimmy Doherty investigates why so many cockles in UK supermarkets come from abroad when he always thought of them as a quintessentially British staple. Kate Quilton takes a trip around Europe's largest marshmallow factory to find out why the sticky treat goes crispy and melty at the same time over the campfire.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Together with children from Redhills Community Primary School, Devon, Kirstie Allsopp makes a scarecrow for her local county show at Chagford. She also learns a thing or two from 14-year-old Sarah Whitely about how to show a saddleback pig, and she judges some of the cutest and shortest sheep handlers, aged 6 to 12.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi scours the property market on the Costa de Almeria to find twin sisters Sona and Roopa Modi a holiday home for their budget of £90,000. The search throws up an unexpected dilemma that sees the siblings torn between a number of strong contenders.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Mariam and Johnno restore their chateau's original bread oven for a sourdough weekend. Angel goes bargain hunting to find a unique radiator cover for the chateau's boot room, while Emma and Paul complete the final room in the west wing and theme it after the renowned artist Alphonse Mucha.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in West Yorkshire, where Emma and Martin battle wind and snow while building a stone home on an imposing hilltop.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The sports car-themed PitStop in Hertfordshire hosts the first round, as owners Melvyn and Sindy Rutter try to make a good impression. However, the absence of tea and coffee in the rooms does not go down well with the guests, and Melvyn's after-dinner talk about his collection of Morgan cars fails to spark much interest.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gill and Peter Tydie invite their fellow competitors to stay at the Victory in Mersea, Essex, a four-star B&B with 26 members of staff to run the bar, restaurant and accommodation. A trip to a local fishery gives one contestant an opportunity to annoy his peers, and the discovery of a hole in a bedsheet leaves the hosts feeling uneasy about their chances of success.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding B&B on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. The hoteliers try to entertain their guests by inviting them to take part in an archery contest - which ends in accusations of unfair play.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. One of the hosts gets upset about large underpayments and a couple interrogate their guests about the criticism in their feedback, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is rather taken with a throne salvaged from an exotic nightclub and Simon O'Brien's restoration of a Victorian doll's house fails to go to plan.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel from Birmingham to Braunston, Northamptonshire, to attend a festival of vintage canal boats, aided on the tricky journey by their son Sam and Juliet, Timothy's daughter from his first marriage. Along the way, they meet a 78-year-old boatman, who reveals how he was born on a canal boat, and unveil a plaque dedicated to a woman who dedicated her life to preserving Britain's canals.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Surrey couple Debbie and Simon move into their fairy-tale castle, while Belinda and Lee expand their events portfolio by hosting a classic car weekend.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Henning Stummel and his partner Alice Dawson plan to convert a dilapidated joinery workshop in north London into a contemporary family home and office. They want to preserve the building's original steel frame, but to restore it they will have to take it down, which means carefully extracting it from the neighbours' walls without causing damage to their property - and that's just the start of the couple's problems. Kevin McCloud follows their progress.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In the first episode of a new series, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Rowland family in Stoke who are unsure whether to sell their home or renovate their current property instead. Hospital worker Julie is unhappy with their house and longs to live in a spacious character property, but Allen does not think they will find anything better than what they currently have within in their budget. After a decade long debate, can Phil and Kirstie help the pair make the right decision?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Aaron, 31, is rushed to St George's after overturning his car onto metal railings during a high-speed collision. He has a serious laceration to his face where a spiked railing narrowly missed his eye socket. Cameras also follow Fiona, 65, who is rushed to the hospital after falling over in a restaurant and cutting her knee during her friend Evelyn's 70th birthday party.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 84-year-old Neil who has fallen down the stairs at home, and his wife Jenny reflects on the shock of realising that their life may never be the same again. Orthopaedic registrar Sadia is concerned about a serious leg fracture to 25-year-old Danny, who was knocked off his motorbike, and may have other hidden internal injuries.(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates this look at the programmes that flirted with danger during the decade, including Blue Peter, which carried on filming as a fire broke out live on air, while The Fun of the Fair showed teenage girls riding motorcycles through burning hoops. At the same time government-produced public information films petrified viewers with scary scenarios of possible accidents in the home. Contributors include Robin Askwith, Liza Goddard, Oona King, Clive Anderson, Philippa Perry, Samira Ahmed, Ellie Taylor and Angela Barnes.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Aaron, 31, is rushed to St George's after overturning his car onto metal railings during a high-speed collision. He has a serious laceration to his face where a spiked railing narrowly missed his eye socket. Cameras also follow Fiona, 65, who is rushed to the hospital after falling over in a restaurant and cutting her knee during her friend Evelyn's 70th birthday party.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 84-year-old Neil who has fallen down the stairs at home, and his wife Jenny reflects on the shock of realising that their life may never be the same again. Orthopaedic registrar Sadia is concerned about a serious leg fracture to 25-year-old Danny, who was knocked off his motorbike, and may have other hidden internal injuries.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates craft beer, meeting a couple who gave up jobs in the NHS to pursue their dream lifestyle of brewing and selling ale. Kate Quilton goes in search of the perfect tomato and finds herself in the middle of the world's biggest food fight at a festival in Valencia, and Matt Tebbutt examines why tinned anchovies taste salty when fresh ones don't, visiting a processing plant to find out.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the shortbread competition at the Chagford Show in Devon, as she tries to woo the judges with some creative takes on the classic treat, including using rosemary and lavender as ingredients.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: With retirement five years away, Graham and Janet Gifford, from Wigan, want to buy a holiday home that they can eventually relocate to permanently in the Italian region of Liguria. Armed with a budget of £80,000, Sara Damergi helps the couple navigate the Italian property market.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Surrey couple Debbie and Simon move into their fairy-tale castle, while Belinda and Lee expand their events portfolio by hosting a classic car weekend.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in rural Cambridgeshire with Christine and Frank, a couple determined to build a huge barn-style home fit for big family get-togethers.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and Shirley Boyle, owners of the Old Hall Country House in Madeley, Cheshire, host the week's first round of the B&B competition. The guests are impressed by the 16th-century building, and are taken tap-dancing and to a private fine-dining restaurant, but one of them is dissatisfied with her bedroom's layout and the garnish on her eggs at breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. For the activity they arrange a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. On arrival, the competitors are impressed by the 'pink palace', but things go downhill when a bed is broken and one couple are allocated a room next to the front door. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is surprised by an overpayment and Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley receives tough criticism, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Northamptonshire and Coventry, where Henry discovers a rare limited edition Honda Goldwing motorbike with only 65 miles on the clock.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go to Ireland and travel along the Shannon Erne Waterway, a canal linking Northern Ireland with the Republic of Ireland. Along the way, they try some traditional dancing and folk singing, visit historic sites including Crom Castle and Bloody Pass, and meet a genealogist with a fascinating story to tell.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: In northern France, Wendy and Marcus start renovating their chateau's barn into a space fit for a wedding venue. Debbie and Simon test run a possible new business venture by hosting a Chinese cooking weekend for their visiting family. Meanwhile, in a bid to top up their renovation funds, Belinda and Lee plan to host a fashion show by their swimming pool, but find wet weather threatens to stop play.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud looks at the transformation of a derelict mill cottage in Northumberland into a contemporary family home, the longest-running project ever featured on the programme. Stefan Lepkowski and Ania Shabowska began work in 2006, and their ambitious plans involved restoring the Georgian building, reconstructing a watermill and adding a steel-and-glass atrium, but their budget of £250,000 was completely inadequate.(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Jamie and Claire face a series of unexpected challenges as they establish their homestead on Fraser's Ridge. Period drama, starring Caitriona Balfe and Sam Heughan.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.sub-title: Are You Right There, Father Ted?
programme.desc: The priest offends Craggy Island's Chinese community and pulls out all the stops to convince them that he is not racist - but his efforts end in disaster. Sitcom, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon, Frank Kelly and Pauline McLynn, with Ozzie Yue.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The cleric tries to nurture Chris, the hot favourite to win the King of the Sheep festival, back to top form after he is left shaken by a terrifying encounter. Meanwhile, Dougal becomes convinced Craggy Island is being terrorised by a monster.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond visits Abu Dhabi's Yas Marina circuit to drive the Porsche 918 Spyder, a four-wheel-drive hybrid hypercar capable of astonishing speeds thanks to the 886 horsepower it can generate from its screaming V8 petrol engine and electric motors. Jeremy Clarkson and James May join forces to create a public information film to promote safer cycling, with their efforts presented to a panel of experts including members of Westminster Council and Olympic gold medal-winning rider Chris Boardman. Meanwhile, Jeremy - who has promised to change his name to Jennifer if the Porsche is faster around the test track than the McLaren P1 driven earlier in the series - compares the seventh-generation Volkswagen Golf GTI to BMW's critically acclaimed M135i. programme.desc: Seasoned rocker Alice Cooper, Who Wants to Be a Millionaire? host Chris Tarrant and wildlife presenter Chris Packham compete to have their pet hates banished for ever, on the comedy panel show chaired by Frank Skinner. Their gripes include reality TV stars, balloon animals, parrots, Jedward, Chris Moyles and kids' artwork on fridges.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry asks Alan Davies, Jo Brand, Bill Bailey and Jimmy Carr for more interesting answers on subjects including bears, bulbs and bamboo in the mind-boggling quiz show.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.sub-title: Have I Got a Bit More Old News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Frankie Boyle hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster Julia Hartley-Brewer and Citizen Khan star Adil Ray joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's news stories.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Actor Larry Lamb, comedian David O'Doherty and presenter Lauren Laverne join host Frank Skinner, making the case for their pet hates to be banished for ever. The trio's dislikes include high fives, fancy dress, ostentatious dresses, being 35, fake tan and square plates.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Liza Tarbuck still leads the pack of five comics, as Russell Howard wraps a tie round his head and Tim Vine is left bereft by a small piece of bent metal.(n)

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon is joined by Morgana Robinson, Lolly Adefope and Matt Forde to address worries to do with The Future. What will we look like in the future? Will robots steal our jobs?(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey explains how writing a biography of his ancestor John, the first Duke of Marlborough, changed the way Winston Churchill was seen by the public and politicians, and so paved the way for his return to power. The first volume, published in 1933, reinforced the perception of him as a man past his time. But it was the second instalment, depicting John Churchill's march to the Danube and victory at Blenheim, which began to mark out Winston as the only leading politician who truly knew war.(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates historical evidence of the story of Jesus, examining his execution and his relationship with Mary Magdalene.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How Second World War criminal Adolf Eichmann was tracked down to Buenos Aires, Argentina, in 1961.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: A look at the planet's most incredible metropolitan railways, and how they help people solve distinctly urban problems. Including a project to upgrade New York's transit system.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Royal William Yard in Plymouth, where they are given the chance to cast their eyes over a number of potentially valuable items. On the waterfront, militaria expert Graham Lay has doubts about a pair of doorknobs thought to have belonged to Admiral Horatio Nelson. The team is impressed by a chess set made from parts of the aircraft carrier HMS Ark Royal, and a psychedelic painting of a band that performed at Woodstock intrigues Clive Stewart-Lockhart. Plus, jewellery specialist John Benjamin marvels at a diamond necklace the length of a skipping rope, donated to the city of Plymouth by Lady Astor.(n)

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Cillian Murphy narrates a documentary examining the lives of animals and people pitting themselves against the world's wildest ocean, unveiling the natural forces that underpin life in the Atlantic, from the world's most powerful currents, ferocious hurricanes and huge chains of undersea volcanoes. The first episode looks at the influence of the Gulf Stream, with whales, giant turtles and jellyfish riding the huge current that becomes a migration super-highway and helps warm northern Europe, while fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to exploit its natural fertility.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of omnivores, nature's generalists, revealing their myriad talents in the wild and how some species have adapted to man's encroachment into their habitats by making their homes in cities and towns.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Engineering breakthroughs that allow trains to go faster than ever, including technology that allows the navigation of the winding mountains of Spain at speed.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The hunt for Gestapo head Klaus Barbie and death camp guard John Demjanjuk, dubbed 'The Butcher of Lyon' and 'Ivan the Terrible' respectively, which lasted into the 1980s.(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated closely with the Resistance to hinder the use of military vehicles by the Nazis.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A ruined compound on the edge of a peninsula in Colombia which was once home to a famous outlaw and the strange towers looming over red-tinted waters in a Portuguese landscape.(n)

programme.desc: Tim Dunn follows his family's roots by exploring Metroland, the area around the Metropolitan Railway, and in Great Malvern learns about the woman behind a decorative 1860s station.(n)

programme.desc: Arkwright hatches a plan to get into Nurse Gladys's bedroom by forcing a reluctant Granville to masquerade as a burglar, so the miserly shopkeeper can come to her rescue. Ronnie Barker and Lynda Baron star.(n)

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Roy Clarke's comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason. Tight-fisted shopkeeper Arkwright purchases fire-damaged stock, only to find all the tins have lost their labels.(n)

Meanwhile, the nutrient-rich upwellings create profusions of life in some areas, and the abyssal depths host a world of bizarre creatures in others.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals

Plus, a Lowestoft carer who looks after his wife hopes that moving into their first proper home together will bring them more happiness, and an out-of-work Liverpool man dreams of a life on the ocean waves.(n)

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.sub-title: On Benefits: Depressed, Stressed and Repossessed
programme.desc: Stories of people struggling on benefits. Mark from Cheltenham is a part-time drag queen who lost his Spanish bar after a licensing dispute. Now home and claiming sickness benefits for depression, he is keen to get well enough to work again. Plus, the stories of a widowed grandmother whose life has been tough since her drug-addicted grandson was sent to prison for attacking her, and a 63-year-old joiner who cannot work because of back trouble.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Elly finds out that Claudia has offered to buy the island, Ned decides to take a new direction in life, and Susan and Karl's relationship deteriorates.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Homeless
programme.desc: Self-confessed daddy's girl Kaya spends three nights sleeping rough, convinced that her piano-playing skills and her looks will help her make enough money to pay for a hostel.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Council House Millionaires
programme.desc: Documentary about some of the nation's wealthiest buy-to-let landlords, who have made a fortune by buying up former council homes, and renting them out to those in need. Among those featured is a Manchester property magnate who buys properties in the city's less desirable areas and rents them to students at up to £400 a month for a room, a London estate agent who only deals in former council properties, and a Kent man who grew up in a council house, and recognises the quality of the construction.(n)

programme.title: The Gypsies Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary following travellers' constant struggle to co-exist with settled communities. Carol and her family cannot understand why her community is so hated and vilified, while local man Arthur is on a mission to understand what brings the travellers into parks and public areas - and Carol's caravan is his first stop. Johnny and his family live in a chalet in Essex but his non-traveller wife Andrea has had enough of feeling ostracised and wants to move back into a normal house.(n)

programme.title: Busted in Bangkok
programme.desc: Documentary following Thailand's tourist police, focusing on four of the country's holiday hotspots as officers and volunteers ensure the good times do not get out of hand. A drunken Brit has a bone to pick over a restaurant bill. But clashing with security guards isn't the wisest of moves, and soon the law enforcers are called in to keep the peace. In Pattaya, volunteer Freddie finds a holiday-maker covered in blood. Unfortunately, his attempts to help spark an unexpected reaction.(n)

programme.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery (1997)
programme.desc: A velvet-clad, sex-crazed secret agent from the 1960s agrees to be put into a state of suspended animation after his arch-enemy escapes justice. Thirty years later he is defrosted to do battle with the power-mad villain again, but the mission proves nearly as daunting for him as getting to grips with 1990s social values. Spoof spy adventure, with Mike Myers, Elizabeth Hurley, Mimi Rogers, Michael York and Robert Wagner.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.desc: Catching up with tenants and landlords from previous episodes, including a man living in a damp and mouldy flat, who was unable to sleep in the bedroom due to the horrendous conditions. The programme also revisits a 70-year-old landlady whose tenants stopped paying the rent for a year, and the council's Rogue Landlord Team in Lewisham, who learned where a slum property owner's reputation for a short fuse came from.(n)

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.sub-title: Money's Too Tight to Mention: On Benefits
programme.desc: Catching up with a few familiar faces to find out how their lives have changed. Doncaster man Carl has been living on benefits since a motorcycle accident left him with multiple injuries. Now he has become the drummer in a band for musicians with disabilities. Karen and Paul lost their launderette business and are now rebuilding their home - the only problem is, they believe the house is haunted - and things are looking up for newlyweds Andy and Diane.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Experienced enforcement agent Stewart and trainee Iain head to Blackpool to chase down a builder owing nearly £3,500 to an unhappy customer, while father and son Del and Dael are in Essex to collect over £2,500 owed to a frozen food company. In south London, Del and Brian attempt to recover nearly £10,000 from a debtor who acted as guarantor for a friend's rental agreement, ending in a bizarre stand-off with the debtor's daughter.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.sub-title: Inside Aldi: Britain's Biggest Budget Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the German supermarket has succeeded in the UK, overcoming industry hostility and old-fashioned snobbery to win the public over with its discounted goods. Former staff reveal how Aldi has overcome shoppers' suspicions using a host of clever techniques to cut costs, from multiple barcodes and less time spent at the tills, to their own-brand products - which are intended to look and taste like their big-name counterparts, but cost a fraction of the price.(n)

programme.title: Are They a Con?
programme.sub-title: Ready Meals: Are They a Con?
programme.desc: Paul Connolly and Jean Johansson present a consumer show investigating claims made by shops and advertisers. In the first edition, they examine pre-prepared food, meeting a Birmingham family whose two ready-meal mad kids can't get enough of the microwave. But will they still be fans when Paul shows them some of the things that may be lurking in their lunch? The team also looks at the bacteria that might be hiding inside some of the most popular convenience foods used by millions of families across the UK.(n)

programme.title: Millionaire Age Gap Love
programme.desc: Part one of two. The popularity of age-gap relationships among the super-rich, meeting six millionaire couples who are decades apart. Stories include that of 37-year-old Carolyn, who landed a job at 54-year-old multi-millionaire John's insurance company, little knowing that within a year she would be engaged to her boss and promoted to CEO of Mansfield Town football club. Meanwhile, DIY millionaire Craig had his life turned upside down when he won the UK's first Big Brother. But now, aged 48, nothing could have prepared him for his whirlwind romance with 33-year-old Laura.(n)

programme.desc: A woman is killed by a letter-bomb, and her scientist husband becomes the chief suspect when it is discovered that the couple were planning to divorce, but the victim was intentionally delaying the proceedings.(n)

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: Benny defends a whistle-blowing US Army analyst from a court-martial, but when he refuses to share redacted government secrets with his colleagues, trust issues begin to develop.(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV is helping to bring criminals using weapons to justice. A CCTV operator's instincts lead him to keep an eye on a man acting suspiciously on a train station platform and who then takes a knife out of his pocket and threatens a woman coming towards him. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the fatal stabbing of a taxi driver whose body was left under his vehicle. The finger of suspicion points to a man who took a drunken ride in the cab on the same night - an ex-convict who is also a gifted writer - and his protege, whose fingerprints were found on a book and cigar left at the scene. Saul Rubinek, JK Simmons and Sonia Braga guest star alongside Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Logan struggle with a heavy caseload, including four unrelated murders, a domestic quarrel, and an apparently motiveless killing that leaves them clueless.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baseball star's father is murdered, and police suspect his death may be linked to a gambling addiction. After investigation, it turns out the star's alibi doesn't hold up and he ends up under suspicion himself.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Four murders occur in Manhattan, all of which seem to have been committed by the same sniper. Although the shootings appear random, Briscoe and Green suspect the deaths are linked and discover a connection between one of the victims and a former marine who is an expert shot with the rifle used in the crimes. Ty Burrell (Modern Family) guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A routine investigation brings to light evidence that prompts Briscoe and Logan to pick up the trail of a political fugitive who went underground following a 1971 burglary that left a police officer dead. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lt Van Buren is mugged by two teenagers, but the hold-up does not go according to plan and the officer discharges her weapon, killing one of the youngsters with a shot to the back. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach.(n)

programme.desc: The team joins forces with military investigators to ascertain whether terrorists were responsible for an explosion at a golf course in which a marine colonel was killed. As inquiries proceed, McGee has to use his computer skills to break into secret government files to ensure the murderers are apprehended. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former marine falls to his death from a hotel balcony, and investigations quickly lead to the discovery of another body. As Gibbs and the team dig deeper, they uncover evidence that points to the activities of a vice ring trafficking young girls. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Two FBI agents try to seize control of the team's investigation into the discovery of a perfectly preserved body hidden in a chimney at a marine base. Reluctant to be ousted from the case, Gibbs agrees to work with them - and learns the victim was a serial killer responsible for the deaths of 14 people. Meanwhile, McGee's literary efforts lead to discord with the others.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: Why Did I Go Mad?
programme.desc: For hundreds of years, psychiatry has treated voices and hallucinations as an enemy. But today new scientific and psychological insights into how the brain works are leading to a radical rethink on what such experiences are - and how they should be treated. Cameras follow three people living with voices, hallucinations and paranoia, to explore what causes this kind of phenomena.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Immortal? A Horizon Guide to Ageing
programme.desc: Veteran presenter Johnny Ball looks back over the years that both he and TV series Horizon have been appearing on air to find out what scientists have learnt about how and why people grow old. He charts developments from macabre early claims of rejuvenation to the latest breakthroughs, and asks if the dream of immortality is any closer to coming true.(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Documentary investigating allegations that a lack of proper controls is allowing financial, physical and even sexual abuse to take place within Britain's mosques. Over a year in the making, this programme exposes how worshippers' generosity can be exploited by religious leaders and how extreme groups can assume control of a community.(n)

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)
programme.desc: A gang of lads selling drugs in Darndale in Dublin's Northside sets out to gain more money and power and enter the big leagues of the narcotics trade. Things begin to unravel quickly as not everyone in the area is willing to let them achieve the notoriety they crave without a fight. Thriller, starring John Connors and Fionn Walton.(n)

programme.title: Kidnapping Freddy Heineken (2015)
programme.desc: Five men struggling to make ends meet decide to kidnap a beer tycoon who is one of Europe's richest men. The plan is a success, and they demand the biggest ransom in history - but tensions within the group threaten their chances of escaping the law. Fact-based crime drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Jim Sturgess and Sam Worthington.(n)

programme.title: Run
programme.desc: Recovering heroin addict Richard spends every day struggling to stay off drugs and only one thing spurs him on - the possibility of re-establishing contact with his teenage daughter. Faced with the prospect of never seeing her again, he's torn between returning to his old ways and salvaging the only relationship that matters to him. The third of four dramas telling the stories of seemingly unconnected people facing life-changing decisions, starring Lennie James, Mona Hammond, Darren Raymond and Brad Damon.(n)

programme.title: 12 In a Box (2007)
programme.desc: A group of old schoolfriends is challenged to spend four days in a country house, making no contact with the outside world - with £1million up for grabs if they can make it. However, completing the challenge is made more complicated when one of them drops dead. Comedy, starring Katy Wix and Kenneth Collard.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: The Government threatens to trim the Lords' powers if they rebel again and Lord Strathclyde publishes his review into the powers the upper house have to block legislation. Cameras follow Labour peer Alf Dubs as he attempts to push through an amendment to the Immigration Bill to allow unaccompanied refugee children into the country, while Admiral Alan West isn't happy about a public poll that has voted overwhelmingly to name a new polar research ship Boaty McBoatface and raises a question about it.(n)

programme.desc: Feature-length documentary filmed throughout 2012, providing an in-depth portrait of Elizabeth II during her Diamond Jubilee year. programme.title: Beauty Tools & Accessories

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts an edition based on the subject of history, as Kate Williams, Adam Hart-Davis, Janina Ramirez, Andrew Graham-Dixon, Ben Willbond, Laurence Rickard, Lars Tharp and Tim Wonnacott try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Richard Osman is the man with the answers.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
